áá¯ááºáá²á·áá«áá¾ááºá· Linux áá±á¸ááá±á¬á· IT áá­á¯ááºá¸áá¾á¬ áá­áá¬á¸áááºá·áá²á· Program áá±á¸áá«áá¾ááºá·ð

Course Description
This course provides a comprehensive introduction to Linux operating systems and essential system administration skills. Learners will gain hands-on experience installing, configuring, managing, and troubleshooting Linux systems using the command line and core administrative tools. The course prepares students for entry-level Linux administration roles and further studies in DevOps, Cloud, and System Engineering.

 
Learning Objectives
By the end of this course, learners will be able to:

â¢ Understand Linux concepts, architecture, and distributions
â¢ Install and configure a Linux operating system
â¢ Navigate and manage the Linux filesystem using the CLI
â¢ Manage users, groups, and file permissions
â¢ Install, update, and manage software packages
â¢ Monitor processes, services, and system resources
â¢ Perform basic disk, networking, and security tasks
â¢ Analyze logs and troubleshoot common system issues
â¢ Automate simple administrative tasks using shell scripts
 
Prerequisites
â¢ Basic computer literacy
â¢ No prior Linux experience required
 
Tools & Environment
â¢ Linux Distribution: Ubuntu (preferred)
â¢ Virtualization: VirtualBox / VMware / WSL2 / Cloud VM
â¢ Terminal emulator
â¢ Internet access
 
Course Outline
 
Module 1: Linux Foundations & Installation
Objective: Understand Linux basics and set up the working environment.

â¢ What is Linux? History and open-source philosophy
â¢ Linux vs Windows/macOS
â¢ Linux architecture: Kernel vs User Space
â¢ Popular distributions (Ubuntu, Debian, RHEL, Rocky, Fedora)
â¢ Linux use cases: Servers, Cloud, DevOps
â¢ Linux filesystem hierarchy overview:
o /, /etc, /var, /bin, /home, /root
â¢ Installing Linux:
o VirtualBox / VMware
o WSL2 or Cloud VM
Lab:

â¢ Install Linux and explore the filesystem
 
Module 2: Command Line Interface (CLI) Essentials
Objective: Become confident using the Linux terminal.

â¢ What is the shell? (bash)
â¢ Terminal vs GUI
â¢ Navigation commands:
o pwd, ls, cd, tree
â¢ File and directory operations:
o touch, mkdir, cp, mv, rm
â¢ Viewing files:
o cat, less, head, tail
â¢ Command syntax, options, and arguments
â¢ Getting help:
o man, --help
Lab:

â¢ Navigate directories and manage files via CLI
 
Module 3: File Management, Search & Permissions
Objective: Secure and manage files effectively.

â¢ Linux file types
â¢ File searching:
o find, locate, grep
â¢ File compression & archiving:
o tar, gzip, zip
â¢ Permissions model:
â¢ Read, Write, Execute (rwx)
â¢ Ownership:
â¢ Users vs Groups
â¢ Commands:
o chmod, chown, chgrp
â¢ Intro to special permissions:
â¢ SUID, SGID, Sticky Bit
Lab:

â¢ Secure files using proper permissions
 
Module 4: User & Group Administration
Objective: Manage system access and privileges.

â¢ User accounts and groups
â¢ Important system files:
o /etc/passwd, /etc/shadow, /etc/group
â¢ User and group commands:
o useradd, usermod, userdel
o groupadd, groupdel
â¢ Switching users:
o su, sudo
â¢ Password policies (intro level)
â¢ Principle of least privilege
Lab:

â¢ Create users and assign group-based permissions
 
Module 5: Software & Package Management
Objective: Install and maintain system software.

â¢ What is a package manager?
â¢ Debian-based systems:
o apt, apt-get, dpkg
â¢ Red Hat-based systems:
o dnf, yum, rpm
â¢ Installing, updating, removing packages
â¢ Managing repositories
â¢ Verifying installed software
â¢ System updates and kernel updates
Lab:

â¢ Install, update, and remove system packages
 
Module 6: Process, Service & Task Management
Objective: Control system processes and services.

â¢ Understanding processes and PIDs
â¢ Process monitoring:
o ps, top, htop
â¢ Resource usage:
â¢ CPU, memory, load average
â¢ Process control:
o kill, killall
â¢ Service management with systemctl:
â¢ start, stop, enable, disable
â¢ Scheduling tasks:
o cron, crontab, at
Lab:

â¢ Monitor processes and manage system services
 
Module 7: Disk & Filesystem Management
Objective: Manage storage and disk usage.

â¢ Disk partitions and filesystems:
â¢ ext4, XFS (intro)
â¢ Disk usage monitoring:
o df, du
â¢ Mounting and unmounting filesystems:
o mount, umount
â¢ Persistent mounts:
o /etc/fstab
â¢ Backup basics:
o tar, rsync
Lab:

â¢ Mount storage devices and analyze disk usage
 
Module 8: Networking & Remote Access
Objective: Configure and troubleshoot basic networking.

â¢ Networking fundamentals:
â¢ IP addressing, DNS, gateways
â¢ Network tools:
o ip, ping, ss, netstat, traceroute
â¢ Host configuration:
o hostname
â¢ Intro to SSH:
â¢ Remote access basics
â¢ SSH hardening:
â¢ Key-based authentication
â¢ Disable root login (intro)
Lab:

â¢ Connect and manage a system via SSH
 
Module 9: Logging & Troubleshooting
Objective: Diagnose and resolve system issues.

â¢ Importance of logs
â¢ Log locations:
o /var/log
â¢ Systemd logging:
o journalctl
â¢ Common system issues:
â¢ Disk full, service failure, permission errors
â¢ Troubleshooting methodology
â¢ Reading and understanding error messages
Lab:

â¢ Analyze logs to identify and fix issues
 
Module 10: Basic Linux Security Practices
Objective: Apply foundational security measures.

â¢ Linux security principles
â¢ File and directory security
â¢ Using sudo safely
â¢ Firewall basics:
o ufw or firewalld
â¢ Disabling unnecessary services
â¢ System updates and patching
â¢ Intro to SELinux / AppArmor (high-level)
Lab:

â¢ Configure basic firewall rules and secure a system
 
Module 11: Introduction to Shell Scripting
Objective: Automate simple administrative tasks.

â¢ What is shell scripting?
â¢ Script structure and execution
â¢ Variables and input/output
â¢ Control structures:
o if, loops (basic)
â¢ File permissions for scripts
â¢ Practical admin automation examples
Lab:

â¢ Write simple automation scripts
 
Module 12: Final Review & Practical Project
Objective: Apply all learned skills in a real scenario.

Final Project:
Set up and configure a Linux system with:

â¢ Multiple users and groups
â¢ Secure file permissions
â¢ Installed and managed services
â¢ Basic firewall configuration
â¢ SSH access secured
â¢ Documented admin steps
Assessment:
â¢ Quizzes per module
â¢ Hands-on labs
â¢ Final practical evaluation
